meta.document_type: HUF Real-Data Validation: CL-01, Shannon-Keff Observables, TV Drift Monitoring, Keff-fill Failure-Mode Discovery
meta.sampling: 1 day per week per quarter (weekly samples)
meta.scope: Validates CL-01 (observable demodulation), CL-03 (effective complexity), and TV-based temporal monitoring. Does NOT validate CL-04 (verdict logic), CL-05 (restorative control), or the structural amplifier. CL-05 remains in STORE status.

structural_phases.description: Four distinct structural phases detected in the 2-year dataset

phases.name: Phase 1: Initial diversity peak
phases.character: Highest Keff in the dataset. 81 models active. Legacy models (TOSHIBA MG07ACA14TA) still dominant. Fleet at peak diversity before consolidation.

phases.name: Phase 2: Consolidation drop
phases.character: Sharpest Keff decline (-0.70 from Phase 1). Carrier count drops from 81 to 74 — 7 models retired. April 22 sees a single-day drop of 5 carriers. TOSHIBA MG08ACA16TA becomes dominant. Highest failure rate of any period (16.8/day). This is the only period that looks like a plausible candidate for intervention analysis, though no control objective or success criterion has been defined for Backblaze.

phases.name: Phase 3: Steady state
phases.character: Longest phase — 12 months of structural stability. Keff oscillates in a narrow band around 17.45. Fleet grows by 37k drives but carrier count stays flat at 74-77. This is the fleet's equilibrium. New WDC WUH722222ALE6L4 drives begin arriving in late Q3 2025.

phases.name: Phase 4: Recovery / diversification
phases.character: Keff rebounds toward Phase 1 levels. WDC WUH722222ALE6L4 overtakes TOSHIBA as top carrier in Oct 2025 — a structural regime change. Lowest failure rate and newest fleet composition.

phase_transition_events.impact: Regime change — first non-Toshiba dominant carrier

carrier_lifecycle.finding: The fleet underwent a complete dominant-carrier rotation over 2 years: Toshiba 14TA → Toshiba 16TA → WDC 22ALE. Each transition coincides with a structural phase boundary. The top carrier never exceeds 14% share — no single-model monoculture — though the fleet remains strongly concentrated relative to the uniform simplex baseline (TV ≈ 0.71, Gini ≈ 0.83).

correlation_keff_vs_failure.interpretation: Weak negative — Keff does not predict daily failure rate. Structural diversity is not a short-term failure predictor.

correlation_tv_vs_failure.interpretation: Weak positive — slightly higher failure rates when TV is higher (more concentrated fleet), but effect is minimal.

failure_analysis.finding: Q3 2024 had the highest failure rate (0.0069% per drive-day) coinciding with the consolidation phase. Q4 2025 has the lowest (0.0029%) coinciding with the newest fleet composition. But the Keff-failure correlation is too weak to claim causation. Failure rate appears driven by drive age, not carrier diversity.

keff_fill_paradox_confirmed.description: The 2-year data confirms the Keff_fill normalization paradox discovered in single-day analysis.
keff_fill_paradox_confirmed.evidence: Carrier count ranged from 72 to 81 over the dataset. When n dropped from 81 to 72, Keff_fill increased mechanically even though raw Keff was falling. Keff_fill is confounded by carrier count changes and cannot be used for temporal tracking.
keff_fill_paradox_confirmed.recommendation: Use raw Shannon-Keff or TV distance for temporal CL-03 monitoring. Reserve Keff_fill for within-snapshot comparisons where n is fixed.

what_real_data_tells_cl05: The Backblaze fleet is structurally stable — 8.12% Keff variation over 2 years. A restorative controller would have little to correct in this regime.

Phase 2 (consolidation) is the only period that looks like a plausible candidate for intervention analysis — Keff dropped 0.7 over 6 months while failure rates rose. However, no control objective or success criterion is defined, so 'useful' cannot yet be assessed.
The gain schedule must handle both steady-state and consolidation regimes. A fixed sigmoid cannot distinguish these.
TV distance is more stable than Keff_fill for cross-temporal comparison because it is not confounded by carrier count changes.
The dominant-carrier rotation (Toshiba→WDC) is a structural regime change that Keff alone does not capture. An identity-aware detector may be needed alongside scalar complexity.
IMPORTANT: This analysis audits gain-schedule inputs and structural behavior. It does NOT demonstrate CL-05 restorative control in operation. CL-05 remains in STORE status.

gain_schedule_calibration.note: These are empirical operating ranges. Any sigmoid midpoint should be within these ranges, not at arbitrary values like 0.5.

pll_compliance.total_drives_processed: ~31 million drive-day records across 112 sample days
pll_compliance.data_source: Backblaze public SMART data, Q1 2024 through Q4 2025
pll_compliance.no_narrative_numbers: Every threshold, range, and percentage is computed from CSV data

what_this_data_supports: CL-01 works on real fleet data — carrier extraction, simplex check, distributional computation
Shannon-Keff as canonical is the right house definition (KCN-001 validated)
TV is the better default temporal / control-input metric than Keff_fill when carrier count changes
Keff is a structural monitor, not a short-term failure predictor (r = -0.06)
Identity-blindness is real: dominant-carrier rotation can happen without a dramatic scalar alarm
The Keff_fill normalization paradox is a genuine failure mode for cross-domain gain scheduling
what_this_data_does_not_support: Full CL-04 verdict validation — no verdict logic was operationally tested
Full CL-05 restorative validation — no closed-loop control was demonstrated
'Operational phase' in the strong sense — one domain validated is first-contact, not operational deployment
'Calibrated instrument' for the whole HUF PLL stack — CL-01 through CL-03 observables are validated, the control layers are not
The structural amplifier — no multi-domain amplification was tested
validation_scope.attribution: Scope framing adopted from ChatGPT RE-024f review (originally misattributed to Gemini — corrected after Peter revealed the model swap)
